name: Bitbucket Server MCP Server
what_it_does: Bitbucket Server (Data Center) MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with self-hosted Atlassian Bitbucket — cloning repositories, reading code, creating and reviewing pull requests, managing branches, accessing build status, and integrating Bitbucket's self-hosted git platform into AI-driven code review and DevOps workflows.
use_cases: Reading code and repositories from Bitbucket Server from code review agents
Creating and reviewing pull requests from code collaboration agents
Accessing PR comments and diff information from review workflow agents
Querying build and pipeline status from CI/CD monitoring agents
Managing branch policies and access controls from DevOps governance agents
Generating PR descriptions and review summaries from AI writing agents
not_for: Bitbucket Cloud users (this is for self-hosted Bitbucket Server/Data Center)
GitHub or GitLab users (use the corresponding GitHub/GitLab MCPs)
Teams needing full DevOps platform automation (use Jira + Bitbucket + Jenkins together)
best_when: An enterprise team running Bitbucket Server or Data Center needs AI-assisted code review and DevOps automation — for self-hosted git environments common in regulated industries.
avoid_when: You use Bitbucket Cloud (different API), GitHub, GitLab, or other git platforms.

auth.notes: Bitbucket Server personal access token (HTTP access token) recommended over username/password. Configure BITBUCKET_BASE_URL, BITBUCKET_TOKEN environment variables. Set minimum required permissions on the access token.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — Bitbucket Server/Data Center requires Atlassian license
pricing.paid_tiers: Bitbucket Data Center: annual license based on users (~$1500+/year for 25 users)
pricing.notes: Bitbucket Server/Data Center requires Atlassian commercial license. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted Bitbucket: no external rate limits; local network latency applies

known_agent_gotchas: Bitbucket Server REST API differs from Bitbucket Cloud API — verify API version compatibility
Self-hosted URL must be configured per deployment — no default cloud endpoint
Bitbucket Server version matters: API capabilities vary between versions (5.x, 7.x, 8.x)
Personal access tokens (HTTP access tokens) preferred over username/password for MCP
HTTPS certificate verification: self-hosted instances may use self-signed certs — configure trust
API paginator may differ between REST API versions — implement proper pagination
agent_readiness.security_notes: Self-hosted deployment — data stays within your infrastructure. HTTP access token preferred over password. HTTPS required (configure trust for self-signed certs). Token with minimum required permissions.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted. HTTP access token. HTTPS required. Code stays on-premises. Minimum permission token for agents.
